Blechnum, commonly known as rock ferns or strap ferns, is a genus of ferns in the family Blechnaceae. Blechnum ferns have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including the potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Blechnum ferns include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to their medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Blechnum ferns include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, such as gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems.
